summary: Gruber documents a Safari compatibility issue with Skype's 'callto:' URI scheme, where including double slashes after the scheme breaks phone number links but not username links. He provides the fix (omit the slashes) and notes that the no-slash format is likely canonical, consistent with how 'mailto:' URIs work. Firefox handles both formats fine, but Safari is picky. The post serves as practical developer guidance for anyone building Skype integration into web pages.
key_points: Safari fails silently on 'callto://' URIs containing phone numbers, though username-based URIs work fine with slashes
The correct format omits slashes after the scheme ('callto:+15558675309'), consistent with 'mailto:' URI conventions
Firefox handles both formats gracefully while Safari does not
MSDN documentation confirms the no-slash format as canonical
money_quotes: You click them and nothing happens.
I have no idea which format is "correct", but given that "mailto:" URIs have never used slashes, my money is on the no-slash format being canonical.
Or, as we call it in the Web 2.0 world, a "Skype mash-up".
key_insight: When implementing 'callto:' URIs for Skype integration, omit the double slashes after the scheme to ensure Safari compatibility, following the same convention as 'mailto:' links.
